patch.value: GH-496: extension flags declared list:true don't accumulate repeated occurrences — host maps to scalar, Commander last-wins drops values

pm ops scan --repos a --repos b silently scans only b; --repos a,b works. The extension declares list:true in its flag contract and documents 'comma-separated or repeatable', but the host's option mapping registers a scalar option, so Commander's default last-wins overwrites earlier occurrences. Every extension advertising a repeatable flag inherits silent data loss — the worst failure mode for agents composing flags incrementally. Fix: when the declared FlagDefinition has list:true, register a collect-style reducer accumulating repeats into an array (merging with comma-splitting like core comma-list flags), or reject repeated occurrences with a clear error. Repro 100% in GH-496 (pm-ops 2026.7.10, but host-side). Related: pm-ltbr added the list field to FlagDefinition; pm-cf1u was the same last-wins trap for core --tag.

Repeated occurrences of an extension flag declared list:true accumulate into an array (parity with core comma-list flags, comma form still works); regression test covers repeated+comma+mixed forms; contract docs state the repeatable behavior; GH-496 closed
